en: Implementation of heat and UV measures at Geosphere heat warning level ≥2 must occur.\nHeat Protection Ordinance (Heat-V), Section 3
An action program with technical, organizational, and personal heat and UV measures must be established based on risk assessment.\nHeat Protection Ordinance (Heat-V), Section 4(1)(1), 5(1-3)
Control Measures (Elimination, Engineering, Administrative, and/or Personal Protective Equipment)
Emergency first aid measures for heat-related symptoms must be defined.\nHeat Protection Ordinance (Heat-V), Section 4(1)(2)
Heat and UV measures implementation at Geosphere heat warning level ≥2 must occur.\nHeat Protection Ordinance (Heat-V), Section 4(4)

Information on hazards from heat and natural UV radiation, health-related symptoms, protective measures, and heat warnings and UV index must be provided.\nHeat Protection Ordinance (Heat-V), Section 7(1-5)
Heat exposure medical examinations must address activities involving significant heat strain. Medical evaluation must consider thermal stress.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 49(2)
